pattern: A min-heap keyed on (value, index) plus a marked-set lets each query greedily consume the smallest still-unmarked elements while a running total tracks the leftover sum.
hints: After each query you only need the sum of the elements that remain unmarked, so keep a running total and subtract whenever you mark something.
To repeatedly pull the smallest-value, smallest-index unmarked elements, use a heap ordered by (value, index) and skip entries that were already marked.

Maintain total = sum(nums), a boolean marked array, and a heap of (value, index). For each query mark the given index (subtract if newly marked), then pop up to k unmarked heap entries, marking each and subtracting its value. Record total after the query.

edge_inputs.description: single element, single query marks everything
query marks an already-marked index then k=0, total unchanged
k larger than remaining unmarked marks all of them
all equal values, several queries draining the array

stress.description: large random array with random index/k queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 100001) for _ in range(n)]
    m = n
    queries = [[rng.randrange(0, n), rng.randrange(0, n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [nums, queries]
stress.note: m == n; indices and k in [0, n-1]

solutions.python:
import heapq
class Solution:
    def unmarkedSumArray(self, nums, queries):
        n = len(nums)
        marked = [False] * n
        heap = [(nums[i], i) for i in range(n)]
        heapq.heapify(heap)
        total = sum(nums)
        ans = []
        for idx, k in queries:
            if not marked[idx]:
                marked[idx] = True
                total -= nums[idx]
            cnt = 0
            while cnt < k and heap:
                v, i = heapq.heappop(heap)
                if marked[i]:
                    continue
                marked[i] = True
                total -= v
                cnt += 1
            ans.append(total)
        return ans
